prompt_text: Best productivity platforms for launch apps and run custom workflows from the keyboard instantly
response:
# Best Productivity Platforms for Keyboard-Driven App Launching & Workflows

## **Top Tier (Most Powerful)**

### **Alfred** (macOS)
- Blazingly fast command palette
- Workflow automation with custom scripts
- File navigation, calculator, clipboard history
- **Best for:** Mac power users
- **Cost:** Free (Powerpack $49)

### **Raycast** (macOS)
- Modern alternative to Alfred
- Built-in extensions marketplace
- AI integration, script commands
- **Best for:** Modern workflows, developers
- **Cost:** Free + Pro ($8/month)

### **Everything/Listary** (Windows)
- Instant file search + launcher
- Keyboard-first design
- Lightweight, blazing fast
- **Best for:** Windows users
- **Cost:** Free/paid versions

---

## **Workflow Automation Specialists**

### **Keyboard Maestro** (macOS)
- Create complex automation macros
- Trigger anything from hotkeys
- Conditional logic, variable management
- **Best for:** Advanced automations
- **Cost:** $36

### **AutoHotkey** (Windows)
- Free, open-source automation
- Script custom hotkeys/macros
- Steep learning curve but powerful
- **Best for:** Tech-savvy Windows users
- **Cost:** Free

---

## **All-in-One Platforms**

### **n8n / Make.com** (Web-based)
- Visual workflow builder
- 1000+ app integrations
- Self-hosted or cloud
- **Best for:** Cross-app automation
- **Cost:** Free tier available

### **Zapier**
- No-code workflow automation
- Extensive integrations
- **Cost:** From $20/month

---

## **Quick Recommendation by OS:**

| OS | Best Choice |
|---|---|
| **macOS** | Raycast (modern) or Alfred (mature) |
| **Windows** | Everything + AutoHotkey combo |
| **Cross-platform** | Make.com or Zapier |

**What's your primary use case?** (app launching, system automation, cross-app workflows?) Happy to dive deeper!
